title: Chiranjeevi Yojana (Rajasthan) — ₹25 Lakh Health Cover
meta_description: Chiranjeevi Yojana Rajasthan 2026 — ₹25 lakh cashless health insurance for all Rajasthan families. Registration, hospital list, claim.

hero.one_liner: Rajasthan's universal health insurance — ₹25 lakh per family per year for cashless treatment at 1,000+ empanelled hospitals across India. Now rebranded as Mukhyamantri Ayushman Arogya Yojana.

what_is_it: Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana (officially rebranded as Mukhyamantri Ayushman Arogya Yojana in February 2024) is Rajasthan's flagship universal health insurance scheme providing ₹25 lakh per family per year for cashless hospitalization. Launched in May 2021 by Chief Minister Ashok Gehlot, it's one of India's most comprehensive state health schemes — covering 1,798 medical procedures and packages.

What makes Chiranjeevi/MAAY exceptional: (1) ₹25 lakh cover is 5× more generous than Ayushman Bharat's ₹5 lakh, (2) It covers virtually ALL Rajasthan families — no income restrictions for the paid category, (3) Treatment accessible at empanelled hospitals across India (Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, not just Rajasthan), (4) Includes 5 days pre-hospitalization expenses (tests, scans before admission) and 15 days post-hospitalization (recovery, medicines after discharge), (5) No age limit, no waiting period for most emergencies.

For NFSA/BPL/Sambal families, registration is completely FREE — the state government pays the ₹3,000-5,000 actual insurance premium. For non-NFSA families (general/APL category), the annual premium is just ₹850 per family — irrespective of family size. This means a joint family of 8 members gets ₹25 lakh cover for ₹850/year, a cost-effectiveness unmatched globally. This generous subsidization reflects the BJP government's continuation of the Congress-era scheme with expanded coverage.

Over 1.35 crore families (approximately 85% of Rajasthan's population) are registered under Chiranjeevi as of 2026. The scheme has processed millions of claims for treatments ranging from appendicitis to organ transplants, heart bypass surgeries to cancer chemotherapy. The rebranding to MAAY in 2024 was administrative; all benefits, coverage, and registration remains unchanged.

eligibility.label: Who can register
eligibility.value: ALL families residing in Rajasthan — no income limit, no age limit, no family size limit for paid category

eligibility.label: Free category
eligibility.value: NFSA (ration card holders), BPL families, Sambal card holders, COVID orphans, transgender persons — zero premium, state govt pays ₹3,000-5,000

eligibility.label: Paid category
eligibility.value: All other Rajasthan residents — ₹850/year for entire family, covering all ages from newborn to elderly

eligibility.label: Coverage
eligibility.value: ₹25 lakh per family per year for cashless hospitalization. Covers 1,798+ medical packages (surgeries, treatments, diagnostics).

eligibility.label: Geographic Access
eligibility.value: Empanelled hospitals across India — 1,000+ hospitals in Rajasthan, Delhi, Mumbai, and other major cities

eligibility.label: Mandatory Document
eligibility.value: Jan Aadhaar Card (Rajasthan family ID system) — MUST BE OBTAINED FIRST before Chiranjeevi registration

extra_sections.caption: The entire process is cashless — you never pay the hospital directly. The government settles with the hospital.

extra_sections.heading: ₹25 lakh is per family per year — not per person
extra_sections.content: The ₹25 lakh cover is shared among all family members registered under one Jan Aadhaar card. If one family member uses ₹15 lakh for a surgery, the remaining ₹10 lakh is available for other family members that year. The limit resets every year on the registration anniversary date.

extra_sections.heading: 1,798+ Covered Medical Procedures and Packages
content: The Chiranjeevi/MAAY scheme covers an extraordinarily comprehensive list of medical treatments: General Surgery — appendicitis, hernia, gallbladder, thyroid, all major surgeries. Orthopedic — knee replacement, hip replacement, spine surgery, joint reconstruction.
Cardiac — angioplasty, bypass surgery, valve replacement, pacemaker insertion. Neurosurgery — brain tumor removal, spine surgery, neurological interventions.
Cancer Treatment — chemotherapy, radiation therapy, surgery, cancer-related procedures. Dialysis — hemodialysis and peritoneal dialysis for kidney patients.
Organ Transplant — kidney, liver, heart transplant surgeries. Obstetrics — normal delivery, cesarean delivery, pregnancy-related complications.
Pediatrics — all child surgeries and treatments. Diagnostics — CT scans, MRI, ultrasound, PET scan, all laboratory tests during hospitalization.

NOT covered: Outpatient treatments (doctor visits without admission), routine dental work, cosmetic surgery, fertility treatments (IVF/IUI), glasses and hearing aids, pre-existing diseases have 30-day waiting period, and self-inflicted injuries. Maternity is covered if pregnancy was before policy enrollment, but post-delivery contraception procedures have restrictions.

extra_sections.heading: How to Use Chiranjeevi at Hospital — Cashless Treatment Process

steps.title: Find empanelled hospital from chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in hospital list
steps.description: Search by district, hospital name, or treatment type. Over 1,000 hospitals across India are network hospitals. Major private chains (Apollo, Fortis, Max, Manipal) and government medical colleges are included.

steps.title: Visit hospital and present Jan Aadhaar + Chiranjeevi card at TPA desk
steps.description: Every empanelled hospital has a dedicated Chiranjeevi/TPA (Third Party Administrator) desk. Show your Jan Aadhaar Card and Chiranjeevi policy document. Staff verifies your registration status in real-time using online portal.

steps.title: Hospital initiates pre-authorization for planned procedures
steps.description: For planned surgeries (not emergencies), hospital sends a pre-authorization request to the insurance company with patient details, diagnosis, and estimated cost. Approval takes 2-4 hours. Emergency admissions are auto-approved for up to 48 hours before pre-authorization.

steps.title: Receive treatment completely cashless
steps.description: Patient pays ZERO rupees — no admission advance, no deposit required. Hospital submits bills directly to insurance company. Patient pays nothing throughout stay. Even post-discharge medicines for 15 days are covered — just collect from empanelled pharmacies.

steps.title: Discharge and follow-up coverage
steps.description: At discharge, hospital provides a discharge summary and medication list. All medicines for 15 days post-discharge are covered if prescribed during hospitalization. For complications, readmission within 7 days is covered under same episode.

extra_sections.heading: Real Examples — What Patients Actually Paid Under Chiranjeevi
content: Mrs. Geeta (age 52, NFSA family): Hospitalized with pancreatitis.
Hospital admission, ICU stay 5 days, ERCP procedure, medications, all tests. Total hospital bill: ₹1,75,000.
Geeta's out-of-pocket payment: ₹0. Chiranjeevi covered everything.
Plus she paid ₹0 annual premium (NFSA free category).
Rajesh (age 38, general category): Requires angioplasty (stent placement for heart blockage). Total hospital cost: ₹3,25,000.
Rajesh's payment: ₹0 at hospital. He had paid ₹850/year premium for his family of 5.
Cost per person: ₹170/year for complete cardiac care coverage.
Sunita (age 28, general category): Normal delivery at private hospital. Hospital cost: ₹1,25,000 (including anesthesia, ICU stay, newborn care).
Sunita's cost: ₹0. Annual premium paid for family of 3: ₹850.
Effective cost: ₹283 per person for maternal-child health coverage.
Govind (age 62, NFSA family): Cancer diagnosed — requires chemotherapy course + surgery. Hospital bills over 6 months: ₹8,75,000.
Out-of-pocket payment: ₹0. Government paid his premium.
Only his time and emotional burden were his cost. Chiranjeevi ensured financial protection during worst health crisis.

extra_sections.heading: Jan Aadhaar Card — The Gateway to Chiranjeevi
content: Jan Aadhaar is a family ID system unique to Rajasthan — separate from national Aadhaar. It's mandatory for Chiranjeevi registration.
Without Jan Aadhaar, you cannot access Chiranjeevi, even in medical emergencies.
How to get Jan Aadhaar: Visit janaadhaar.rajasthan.gov.in or your nearest E-Mitra Kiosk. Bring: Aadhaar of all family members, latest electricity bill (residence proof), family photo.
Processing: 5-7 days. Fee: ₹0 (free).
You'll receive a Jan Aadhaar card (plastic card) or can download digital version.
Once you have Jan Aadhaar, Chiranjeevi registration takes just 5-10 minutes online or at E-Mitra. If NFSA/BPL: automatically enrolled for free.
If general: pay ₹850 and activate.

extra_sections.heading: Works across India, not just Rajasthan
extra_sections.content: Chiranjeevi/MAAY is valid at empanelled hospitals across India — not limited to Rajasthan. If a Rajasthan family member needs treatment in Delhi or Mumbai, they can use the scheme at any hospital empanelled under Chiranjeevi or Ayushman Bharat. Carry your Jan Aadhaar card and registration receipt.

how_to_apply.title: Get Jan Aadhaar Card if you don't have one
how_to_apply.description: Visit janaadhaar.rajasthan.gov.in or E-Mitra kiosk with Aadhaar + residence proof. 5-7 days processing, completely free.

how_to_apply.title: Register for Chiranjeevi online at chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in
how_to_apply.description: Click 'New Registration' → enter Jan Aadhaar number → verify category (NFSA/BPL/general) → complete form.

how_to_apply.title: If general category, pay ₹850 annual premium
how_to_apply.description: Pay online via net banking, debit card, UPI, or at E-Mitra kiosk. Payment modes: NEFT, RTGS, debit card. No processing charges.

how_to_apply.title: Download digital policy card from portal
how_to_apply.description: After registration (instant for free category, after payment for paid category), download your Chiranjeevi policy document. Keep it in mobile phone or print it.

how_to_apply.title: Use at any empanelled hospital for cashless treatment
how_to_apply.description: Show Jan Aadhaar + policy card at hospital TPA desk. Treatment is cashless. Track claims and benefit usage on the portal.

important_alert.text: Jan Aadhaar Card is ABSOLUTELY MANDATORY for Chiranjeevi registration and usage. Without Jan Aadhaar, even emergency treatments will NOT be covered. If you don't have Jan Aadhaar, register IMMEDIATELY at janaadhaar.rajasthan.gov.in before needing hospitalization.

important_dates.label: Registration
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year at chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in

important_dates.label: Premium renewal
important_dates.value: ₹850/year (paid category) — renew annually before expiry to maintain coverage

important_dates.label: Helpline
important_dates.value: 181 (state helpline) or contact TPA for claim issues

faqs.question: I have both Ayushman Bharat (PMJAY) and Chiranjeevi. Which cover is used first?
faqs.answer: You have both coverages simultaneously. At hospital, typically Chiranjeevi is used first since it has ₹25 lakh limit (higher than Ayushman's ₹5 lakh). If Chiranjeevi limit is exhausted, Ayushman coverage activates. However, hospitals usually use only one scheme per admission to avoid administrative complexity. If treatment cost exceeds both coverages combined (unlikely for most procedures), patient bears additional cost. Having both provides a safety net.

faqs.question: I'm from Rajasthan but working in Delhi. Can I get treatment in Delhi hospitals?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. Chiranjeevi has empanelled hospitals across India — including Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore, and other major cities. Treatment is cashless at ANY empanelled hospital, not just Rajasthan. Many patients get treatment in Delhi/Mumbai for specialized procedures while their Chiranjeevi is active. List of all empanelled hospitals is on chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in.

faqs.question: My treatment cost exceeds ₹25 lakh. What happens?
faqs.answer: Chiranjeevi covers up to ₹25 lakh per family per year. Anything beyond that is patient's responsibility. However, ₹25 lakh covers 99% of medical procedures available in India. Treatments exceeding this are extremely rare and usually experimental. In hardship cases, the Chief Minister's Relief Fund or hospital charity can provide additional support — approach hospital social worker or the CM's office.

faqs.question: How can ₹850 cover a family of 8?
faqs.answer: It's heavily subsidized. Actual insurance premium per family is ₹3,000-5,000. The Rajasthan government pays the difference from state budget. For NFSA families, government pays 100%. This policy choice reflects the government's commitment to universal health coverage. Unlike private health insurance where 1-2 people's family costs ₹8,000-12,000/year, Chiranjeevi covers entire family for ₹850. The state has absorbed the subsidy since 2021.

faqs.question: My claim was rejected by insurance company. What's my appeal process?
faqs.answer: Submit grievance complaint to: (1) Hospital's TPA coordinator, (2) State Health Assurance Agency (SHAA) complaint portal on the website, (3) Helpline 181 with complaint details. You can also escalate to district civil hospital director or health department. Most claim rejections are due to documentation issues, not legitimate denials. Resubmit with complete medical records.

faqs.question: Can I use Chiranjeevi for psychiatric hospitals or mental health treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mental health treatments at recognized psychiatric hospitals are covered under Chiranjeevi. This includes inpatient psychiatric care, de-addiction treatment, electroconvulsive therapy (ECT), and related procedures. OPD psychiatric consultations are not covered (as with all OPD), but hospitalization for mental health is fully covered.

faqs.question: Is fertility treatment (IVF) covered under Chiranjeevi?
faqs.answer: No. Assisted reproductive techniques like IVF, ICSI, IUI are not covered under Chiranjeevi. However, if you have complications from fertility treatment (ectopic pregnancy, ovarian hyperstimulation requiring hospitalization), those emergency treatments are covered. Cosmetic gynecology is also excluded.

ministry: Department of Medical, Health and Family Welfare, Government of Rajasthan
